text: Curates a NEUTRAL artifact brief for the reviewers — gives them the design, constraints, and dropped alternatives but strips the author's stated leaning/conclusion, so the panel is not anchored on the Redis token-bucket choice.
Picks several appropriate adversarial lenses for an architecture decision (e.g. architect, skeptic/simplest-thing, ops/failure, performance/scale) rather than a single generic 'reviewer'.
Sets the reviewers up blind to one another and framed to refute / find what's missing, not to politely 'review'.
Specifies a fixed, comparable per-reviewer output so the results can be synthesized side by side.
Shows the plan (how many agents, which lenses, rough cost) and asks before firing — does not silently spawn a panel or assume consent.
Convenes independent fresh reviewers rather than just delivering its own single critique — the independence/anti-anchoring is the point, not one more opinion from the same context.
